commit.message:
Use active station profile in storage workbench smoke test (#96)

The smoke test was always building sample QSOs with hardcoded
SampleProtoFactory constants (K7DBG, CN87up, etc.) instead of
using the engine's active station profile.

Add an optional StationProfile parameter to RunSmokeTestAsync.
When present, it overwrites StationCallsign and StationSnapshot
on the sample QSO with real profile data. The Razor page passes
the active profile from DebugWorkbenchState. SampleProtoFactory
remains a pure sample generator, unaffected by this change.

Fixes #95

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve the init command with interactive wizard (#84)

* Improve the init command with interactive wizard

Restructure copilotd init into a polished 6-phase interactive wizard:

Phase 1 - Dependencies & Auth: Show copilotd version, display gh/copilot
CLI versions with checkmarks, clarify copilot uses gh authentication.

Phase 2 - Repo Home: Same as before with helper text.

Phase 3 - Global Settings (new): Prompt for max concurrent sessions and
optional default model.

Phase 4 - Default Rule Setup (new interactive): Prompt for author
filtering (any/only me/write access), required labels, yolo mode,
allow-all-tools/urls, and model override. Existing config values are
used as defaults on re-run.

Phase 5 - Repo Selection: Same multi-select with pre-selection of
previously chosen repos on re-run.

Phase 6 - Summary: Config summary table and concrete next-step command
examples.

Also adds GetVersion() to GhCliService and CopilotCliService to
eliminate redundant subprocess calls.

Closes #74

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add confirmation echo lines after each prompt in init wizard

Spectre.Console's SelectionPrompt clears its interactive area after
selection, making previous answers invisible. Add blue echo lines
after each prompt (author filter, labels, yolo, tools/urls, model)
so users can see their progress through the wizard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add requirements_colab.txt and fix Colab-specific issues

- Add requirements_colab.txt that only installs packages missing from
  Colab's default environment (mglearn, dowhy, empiricaldist, stargazer,
  wrds, newsapi-python, plotnine, tables).  Deliberately excludes numpy,
  scipy, requests, and cryptography to avoid:
    • numpy ABI breakage / 'cannot import _center from numpy._core.umath'
      (pre-compiled extensions crash after a numpy upgrade without restart)
    • 'you must restart the runtime' warning after numpy upgrade
    • google-colab/pyopenssl/pydrive2 conflicts from requests/cryptography
      version bumps

- Switch all 16 notebooks (9 lectures + 7 exercises) to install from
  requirements_colab.txt instead of requirements.txt

- 03c_supervised_learning.ipynb + exercise student version:
  Add '!apt-get install -y graphviz -qq' immediately after pip install.
  Colab does not ship the graphviz system binary, so graphviz.Source()
  raised ExecutableNotFound: failed to execute ['dot', ...]

- 05b_deep_learning.ipynb:
  Replace deprecated 'from keras.preprocessing import image' and
  'image.array_to_img(...)' with 'from keras.utils import array_to_img'
  (keras.preprocessing.image was removed in Keras 3.x)

- Also normalise 05b source arrays from single long strings to
  standard line-per-element format (no semantic change)

commit.message:
fix: Authenticode-sign Windows updater bundle

The previous workflow signed the NSIS installer exe after Tauri had
already packaged the unsigned exe into the .nsis.zip updater bundle.
Users updating via the in-app updater would get an unsigned installer,
triggering SmartScreen warnings.

Now the flow is:
1. Authenticode-sign the NSIS installer exe
2. Re-create the .nsis.zip from the signed exe
3. Re-sign the .nsis.zip with Tauri's Ed25519 key

Port @azure/postgresql-auth package from postgres-entra-auth repo (#37647)

## Description

Migrates the standalone
[azure-postgresql-auth](https://github.com/Azure/postgres-entra-auth/tree/main/javascript)
library into the monorepo as `@azure/postgresql-auth` - a new Azure SDK
client package providing Entra ID authentication for PostgreSQL
connections via both `pg` (node-postgres) and Sequelize clients.

### Key changes:
- **Source:** TypeScript implementation with full type safety
- `getEntraTokenPassword()` - acquires Entra ID token for use as
PostgreSQL password
- `configureEntraIdAuth()` - registers Sequelize `beforeConnect` hook
for automatic token-based auth
- **Build:** Uses `warp` build system with 4 targets (browser,
react-native, esm, commonjs)
- **Tests:** 19 unit tests with vitest (all passing)
- **Peer deps:** `pg` (>=8.0.0) and `sequelize` (>=6.0.0) as optional
peer dependencies
- **CI:** New `sdk/postgresql/ci.yml` data-plane pipeline
- **CODEOWNERS:** Updated

### References
- Standalone source:
https://github.com/Azure/postgres-entra-auth/tree/main/javascript
- .NET reference: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/55231

[test] Fix test_frr_config_check to filter non-FRR config files (#23838)

### Description of PR

Summary: Fix `test_frr_config_check` false failures caused by non-FRR
config files in `/etc/sonic/frr/`.

#### What is the motivation for this PR?

`get_frr_config_files()` uses `file.endswith('.conf')` which picks up
ANY `.conf` file in `/etc/sonic/frr/`. On switches with upgrade history,
stale FRR package files (e.g. `support_bundle_commands.conf`) persist
and cause guaranteed false failures since their content never appears in
`vtysh show running-config`.

**Code path proving the bug:**
1. `get_frr_config_files()` -> `ls /etc/sonic/frr` -> includes
`support_bundle_commands.conf`
2. `verify_frr_config_in_running()` -> reads the file -> 952 lines of
non-FRR content (e.g. `PROC_NAME:bgp`, `CMD_LIST_START`, `show bgp
summary`)
3. `is_config_in_running()` -> searches `vtysh show running-config` -> 0
matches -> test fails

**Why the file exists on some switches:**
The FRR debian package installs `support_bundle_commands.conf` to
`/etc/frr/` (see
`src/sonic-frr/frr/tools/etc/frr/support_bundle_commands.conf` in
sonic-buildimage). At runtime, `/etc/sonic/frr/` is bind-mounted over
`/etc/frr/`. If the FRR package was ever installed or upgraded inside a
running container, dpkg wrote the file to the bind-mounted host path.
`/etc/sonic/` survives image upgrades, so the file persists
indefinitely.

**Repro on any switch:**
```bash
sudo cp /etc/frr/support_bundle_commands.conf /etc/sonic/frr/
# Run test_frr_config_check -- guaranteed failure
```

#### How did you do it?

Replaced the `.endswith('.conf')` filter with a whitelist of the 7 FRR
config files that `docker_init.sh` actually generates:

| File | Mode |
|------|------|
| `bgpd.conf`, `zebra.conf`, `staticd.conf`, `vtysh.conf` | separated
mode |
| `bfdd.conf`, `ospfd.conf` | optional daemons |
| `frr.conf` | unified mode |

Source: `dockers/docker-fpm-frr/docker_init.sh` in sonic-buildimage.

#### How did you verify/test it?

KVM integration test on vlab-01 (t0 topology) with stale files injected:

**Setup:** Copied `support_bundle_commands.conf` and `daemons` into
`/etc/sonic/frr/` to simulate a switch with upgrade history.

**Result — file filtering (the fix):**
```
Found FRR config files: ['bgpd.conf', 'staticd.conf', 'vtysh.conf', 'zebra.conf']
Missing configurations in bgpd.conf: []
Missing configurations in staticd.conf: []
Missing configurations in vtysh.conf: []
Missing configurations in zebra.conf: []
Initial check - All configurations are present in running config
```

Stale files (`support_bundle_commands.conf`, `daemons`) correctly
excluded from verification. All 4 FRR daemon configs verified with 0
missing lines.

**Note:** Config reload phase failed with `SwSS container is not ready`
due to KVM testbed lacking minigraph deployment -- this is a
pre-existing testbed state issue unrelated to this code change. The file
filtering fix (the actual bug fix) is fully validated.

#### Any platform specific information?

No -- affects any platform where `/etc/sonic/frr/` contains stale FRR
package files from upgrade history.

#### Supported testbed topology if it's a new test case?

N/A -- existing test, bug fix only. Test runs on t0, t1 topologies
(unchanged).

Signed-off-by: deepak-singhal0408 <deepak-singhal0408@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(generator): align FROM/JOIN aliases and inline ON conditions (#33)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Implements the two formatting changes described in #32.

**ON/USING inline** — join keyword, table ref, alias, and condition are
all on one line. No more `\n ON` break.

**Alias alignment** — when all `FROM`/`JOIN` entries in a block are
simple table refs, aliases are padded so they start at the same column.
The column is `max(keyword_width + table_name_width) + 1`. If any entry
uses a subquery, alignment is skipped for the whole block but ON stays
inline.

**`AS` omitted from table aliases** — the `AS` keyword is dropped
between table name and alias on `FROM`/`JOIN` lines. Column aliases in
`SELECT` lists are unchanged.

## Changes

- `src/jarify/generator.py` — new helpers (`_join_keyword`,
`_table_alias_str`, `_inline_on_sql`, `_table_ref_only_sql`,
`_compute_join_align_width`) and overrides for `join_sql`, `from_sql`;
`select_sql` now sets `_join_alias_col` before delegating to super
- `tests/fixtures/joins/aligned_joins.{input,expected}.sql` — new
fixture covering a multi-table aligned block
- Updated `bare_join`, `multi_join`, `real_world`, and `basic_cte`
expected fixtures for inline ON and dropped `AS`
- `tests/test_formatter.py` — new `TestJoinFormatting` class (6 unit
tests)
- `docs/sql-style-guide.md` — updated "Table aliases" rule; added "JOIN
formatting" section

Resolves #32

---------

commit.message:
feat(iac): add snapshosts for microos-based vms for k3s cluster (#45)

* feat(iac): add snapshosts for microos-based vms for k3s cluster

I decided to migrate from hard and costly talos based cluster withotu
proper autoscaling features in the terraform modules, to the lightweight
version of kubernetes from SUSE, called k3s.

That switch allows me to keep the kubernetes benefits without the
caveats of maintaing huge cluster. K3S uses much less resoruces than k8s
control-plane, so it's better to current side-project vibe of my
infrastructure.

In later stages it will restore the previous setup in most way.
Hopefully it will be much cheaper.

* fix(iac): apply sugestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): do not run build snapshot on the pr level

We shouldn't run the snapshots builder on the PR level, as we want to
avoid building the snapshot upon PR creation.

* fix(iac): resolve incorrect url creation when calling hcloud

* fix(iac): use underscores instead of hyphens in output key

* fix(iac): Add timeout for 10 minutes upon creation

* fix(iac): use accept-new for host key checking

* fix(iac): add SSH key management and fix authentication issues

- Generate and upload temporary SSH key for server authentication
- Remove rescue mode in favor of SSH key-based authentication
- Add comprehensive cleanup of servers, SSH keys, and temp files
- Fix API path issues (remove leading slashes)
- Add jq dependency validation
- Fix SELinux path typo (/ver/lib -> /var/lib)
- Use StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new for better security

This fixes the script hanging on SSH connections and ensures proper
resource cleanup on both success and failure scenarios.

* fix(iac): use rescue mode and create NetworkManager directory

- Re-enable rescue mode which has qemu-img pre-installed
- Create /etc/NetworkManager directory before touching config file
- Update step numbering to reflect actual process (1/9 through 9/9)

* fix(iac): install qemu-utils in rescue environment before writing image

Hetzner's rescue mode doesn't have qemu-img pre-installed, so we need
to install qemu-utils package before attempting to convert and write
the MicroOS image to disk. Also use sync before reboot for safety.

* fix(iac): use sysrq trigger for immediate reboot after disk write

After writing MicroOS image to /dev/sda, the running system's binaries
are corrupted and cannot be executed. Use kernel sysrq trigger for
immediate reboot instead of sync/sleep/reboot which will fail.

* fix(iac): use background reboot with nohup after disk write

Use nohup with sleep and reboot -f in background to ensure the SSH
connection closes cleanly before reboot. Increase wait time to 10s
to give the system more time to reboot into MicroOS.

* fix(iac): enable sysrq and trigger kernel reboot after image write

Run qemu-img in background with kernel sysrq reboot trigger. Enable
sysrq first, then trigger immediate kernel reboot after image write.
Increase wait time to 30s for image write and reboot to complete.

* fix(iac): remove background process and use direct sysrq reboot

Remove background subshell that was causing the script to hang.
Execute sysrq reboot directly after image write completes.
Change set -ex to set -e to avoid verbose output during reboot.

* fix(iac): separate image writing from reboot to avoid corruption

Split the image write and reboot into two separate SSH commands:
1. First command writes the image completely (with normal run_remote)
2. Second command triggers reboot only (with run_remote_disconnect_ok)

This prevents the reboot from interrupting the image write process
and ensures binaries aren't corrupted before the reboot trigger.

* fix(iac): inject SSH key into MicroOS image before reboot

After writing the MicroOS image to disk, mount the root partition
and inject the SSH authorized_keys from the rescue system. This
ensures SSH access works after rebooting into MicroOS.

Mount /dev/sda3 (MicroOS root partition), create .ssh directory,
copy authorized_keys, and set proper permissions before unmounting.

* fix(iac): write image to temp file, inject SSH key, then write to disk

Convert qcow2 to raw format in /tmp first, mount it via loop device,
inject SSH key, then write the modified image to /dev/sda using dd.

This prevents filesystem corruption by not mounting /dev/sda after
it has been overwritten by the image.

* fix(iac): improve loop device partition detection and add debugging

- Use losetup --show -P to properly create partition devices
- Add partprobe to ensure kernel recognizes partitions
- Add debug output to list partitions
- Try both p3 and p2 for root partition
- Add error handling if no partition found

* fix(ci): failing script for creation of snapshots

The script was not correctly booting into the rescue mode, now it's
fixed and working fine

commit.message:
Fix crossed book: snapshot catch-up MIN, diagnostic stats

Root cause: TrackSnapshotHeader used MAX(LastMsgSeqNumProcessed)
across instruments instead of MIN. Instruments captured early in the
snapshot cycle lost incrementals between MIN and MAX, causing phantom
orders and persistently crossed books.

Fix: use MIN so all instruments receive all incrementals after the
earliest snapshot capture point. Redundant replays for later-captured
instruments are handled gracefully by existing order logic.

Additions:
- /stats endpoint: order counters, gap stats, crossed book list
- BookManager diagnostic counters (adds/updates/deletes/deleteNotFound)
- ChannelHandler counters (processed/duplicates/gaps)
- Crossed-book transition detection with logging (first 20)
- ChannelHandlerProvider wired in Program.cs

Add script to migrate legacy JSONL raw-layer tables to Iceberg

by Airbyte before the platform standardized on Iceberg format via dlt.  This
script converts those tables so they are accessible via the StarRocks
ol_data_lake_iceberg external catalog.

- Positive eligibility check: only migrates TextInputFormat + JSON SerDe tables
- Saves original Glue table definition and restores it if Iceberg creation fails
- Validates row count from Iceberg snapshot after write
- Uses pa.fs.S3FileSystem (PyArrow-native) for reliable dataset reads
- Warns about non-Iceberg, non-JSONL tables without touching them

Usage:

  uv run python bin/migrate-raw-jsonl-to-iceberg.py --env qa --no-dry-run

commit.message:
Fix excess spike indices in CurationV1/SpikeSorting get_sorting by removing invalid spikes (#1564)

* Initial plan

* Fix invalid spike_times in CurationV1 by clipping out-of-bounds indices

When spike times (stored in seconds) are converted back to sample indices
using np.searchsorted, floating-point precision issues can cause the
returned index to equal n_samples (one past the end), which SpikeInterface
rejects with a ValueError. This fix clips the indices to [0, n_samples-1]
and logs a warning when this occurs, in both CurationV1.get_sorting() and
SpikeSorting.get_sorting().

Fixes #1092

fix: Update enable_auto_tracing defaults for hosted agent compatibility (#474)

## Summary

Updates enable_auto_tracing() so a bare call works correctly with Azure
AI Foundry hosted agents and also covers LangGraph callback-manager
construction paths used by invoke() and ainvoke().

## Changes

| Area | Before | After | Reason |
|------|--------|-------|--------|
| enable_content_recording default | True | False | Content recording
should be opt-in for privacy and security |
| provider_name default | None | azure.ai.openai | Uses the canonical
GenAI provider name for Azure OpenAI spans |
| auto_configure_azure_monitor default | True | False | Hosted agents
configure their own TracerProvider; calling configure_azure_monitor()
can create conflicting providers and duplicate or lost spans |
| LangGraph callback-manager hooks | BaseCallbackManager.__init__ only |
BaseCallbackManager.__init__ plus LangGraph callback-manager helper
factories | Ensures tracer injection also covers LangGraph helper paths
used by invoke() and ainvoke() |

Note: message_keys is unchanged. The tracer already defaults to
[messages] when it is not explicitly provided.

## Additional updates

- normalize azure_openai and azure-openai aliases to azure.ai.openai
before assigning the default provider name
- patch LangGraph callback-manager helper functions in
langgraph._internal._config, langgraph._internal._runnable, and
langgraph.pregel.main so the tracer is injected into managers created
for graph execution
- add regression tests covering the new default resolution path and the
LangGraph async callback-manager helper patch
- clarify in the docstring that non-hosted apps must pass
auto_configure_azure_monitor=True or configure telemetry themselves

## Motivation

Hosted agent samples previously had to pass explicit parameters to avoid
conflicts, especially auto_configure_azure_monitor=False. In addition,
relying only on BaseCallbackManager.__init__ is brittle when LangGraph
builds callback managers through helper functions on the invoke and
ainvoke paths.

After this change, enable_auto_tracing() with no arguments works better
in hosted environments and explicitly patches the LangGraph
callback-manager factory path as well. In non-hosted scripts or
notebooks, either pass auto_configure_azure_monitor=True or configure a
TracerProvider and exporter separately.

## Backward Compatibility

All parameters still accept explicit values. Environment variable
overrides still work. The behavioral change is limited to the defaults
used when parameters and relevant environment variables are not
supplied, plus broader tracer injection coverage for LangGraph
callback-manager construction paths.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(issue-triage): improve agentic workflow prompt and fix label application (#4115)

* fix(issue-triage): use add-labels safe output for label application

The issue triage workflow was using update-issue to apply labels, but
the update_issue tool description only mentions 'status, title, or body'
- it does not mention labels. The agent therefore never called it for
label application, resulting in labels being mentioned in the triage
comment but never actually applied to the issue.

Fix:
- Add add-labels safe output (max: 10) to the frontmatter
- Update Critical Label Rules to use add-labels instead of update-issue
- Update Safe Outputs section with explicit combined case: call both
  add-labels AND add-comment (the most common scenario), with a clear
  warning that listing labels in the comment body alone does NOT apply them

Recompiled issue-triage.lock.yml to include the new add_labels handler.
